summary: Scorching Scarlet>Fire, tight jams and strong second‑set flow
blurb: The show delivers a fiery Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain segue followed by a seamless Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World stretch. Candyman, Bird Song and Deal close a solid first set while the second set builds to powerful jams culminating in a soaring Morning Dew. Jerry’s guitar shines, Brent’s keyboards sparkle, and the dual drummers drive an energetic groove.
review: On July 31 1982 at Manor Downs, the Dead turned up the heat with a first set that balanced classic rockers and tight country‑rock moments – Alabama Getaway into The Promised Land, a bright Candyman, soaring Bird Song and a punchy Deal. The real fireworks erupted in the second set: Scarlet Begonias melted straight into Fire on the Mountain, then surged through an inspired Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World sequence that flowed into Space, drums and a seamless Uncle John’s Band → Truckin' transition before launching into a heartfelt Morning Dew. Jerry Garcia was “crisp” and “top‑form,” delivering scorching leads on Scarlet>Fire and expressive vocals on Morning Dew, while Phil Lesh laid down “bass bombs” that kept the groove throbbing. Brent Mydland added rich vocal harmonies on Candyman and his keyboards rang clear in the Estimated/Eyes jam. Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann were described as an “extra step above” the usual rhythm section – eight‑armed drummers who drove the jams with crystal‑clear precision. The energy never waned, making this a standout summer show that rewards repeated listening.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Strong positive sentiment across five recordings (all praising the Scarlet>Fire sequence, Estimated/Eyes jam, tight playing and overall energy) with one mixed review that does not detract from the consensus. No consistent negative remarks were found, so a high positive adjustment is applied.

best_recording.reason: Solid soundboard capture with generally clear, well‑balanced mix; highlights the tight playing and key jams while only showing occasional static, making it the most complete and reliable source.

key_highlights: Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain sequence ignites the second set
Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World jam flows seamlessly into Space and drums
Candyman and Bird Song showcase a tight, energetic first‑set performance
Morning Dew delivers an emotional climax to close the show

band_performance.Jerry: Crisp, top‑form guitar work throughout; scorching tone on Scarlet>Fire and expressive vocals on Morning Dew; strong leads on multiple songs.
band_performance.Phil: Powerful bass presence described as a beast firing away with “bass bombs”; occasional lighter moments noted but overall solid foundation.
band_performance.Bob: Vocals and rhythm guitar highlighted as great, especially on early‑set numbers; no major criticisms reported.
band_performance.Brent: Provides tight vocal harmonies on Candyman and shines in the Estimated/Eyes jam; keyboard textures are clear and contribute to the flow of the second set.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums described as an “extra step above” usual standard, driving jams with crystal‑clear precision.
band_performance.Billy: Partnered with Mickey for a dual‑drum powerhouse; praised for tight, eight‑armed groove that fuels the entire performance.
